imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a slender figure wearing a lime-green anorak and sapphire earrings, with a glowing silver medallion. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Kael sits cross-legged on a low pedestal in a dimly lit, futuristic crypt, assembling a complex jigsaw puzzle, a bluejay pecking at a faucet in the background, close-up, eye-level. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael in a dynamic kungfu stance, deflecting an attack from an 'unemotional frank hunter' with glowing red knuckles, industrial hailstone falling around them, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael on a futuristic bicycle, deploying 'acrylic spring traps' and spraying 'herbs mixture' from a hosepipe at leaking, damaged robotic jacks in a gully landscape, low-angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael stands victorious over a defeated, 'blackened monster' resembling a large elephant, a glowing 'clover' medallion visible on his chest, triumphant high-angle.

definitions.definition: Someone who practices mysticism.
To be a master.
To become the master of; to subject to one's will, control, or authority; to conquer; to overpower; to subdue.
To learn to a high degree of proficiency.
definitions.example: It took her years to master the art of needlecraft.

definitions.definition: To own; to possess.
(especially of a musical performance) To make a master copy of.
(usually with in) To earn a Master's degree.
definitions.example: He mastered in English at the state college.

definitions.definition: Becoming professional in the use of something; having mastery of.
The process by which an audio recording is mastered.
A heavy weatherproof jacket with an attached hood; a parka or windcheater.
A geek or nerd, possibly originally either a train spotter or a fan of off-shore pirate radio.
One who pursues.
The party who initiates a lawsuit; a plaintiff.
The base or foot of a column, statue, vase, lamp.
A place of reverence or honor.
definitions.example: He has put his mother on a pedestal. You can't say a word against her.

definitions.definition: A casting secured to the frame of a truck of a railcar and forming a jaw for holding a journal box.
A pillow block; a low housing.
(bridge building) An iron socket, or support, for the foot of a brace at the end of a truss where it rests on a pier.
(steam heating) a pedestal coil, group of connected straight pipes arranged side by side and one above another, used in a radiator.
To set or support on (or as if on) a pedestal.
Empty; void; lacking meaningful content.
Showing a lack of thought or intelligence; vacant
A species of North American jay with blue feathering from the top of its head to midway down its back, Cyanocitta cristata.
One of the blue-marked species of the Old World bird genus Coracias.
A soprano and melody wind instrument in the modern orchestra and wind ensemble. It is a smaller instrument and generally made of grenadilla wood. It is a member of the double reed family.
A device, usually hinged with a striking plate, used for knocking on a door.
A person who knocks.
A critic; one who disparages.
A person who knocks (denigrates) something.
(usually in the plural) A woman's breasts.
(especially Cardigan) A dwarf, goblin, or sprite imagined to dwell in mines and to indicate the presence of ore by knocking.
A mechanical device in a pinball table that produces a loud percussive noise.
A person who is strikingly handsome or otherwise admirable; a stunner.
A large cockroach, especially Blaberus giganteus, of semitropical America, which is able to produce a loud knocking sound.
A large, boulder-shaped outcrop of bedrock in an otherwise low-lying landscape, chiefly associated with a mélange.
One who defaults on payment of a wager.

A synthetic rubber, a polymer of chloroprene, commonly used in wetsuits, laptop sleeves, orthopedic braces, electrical insulation, liquid and sheet-applied elastomeric membranes and flashings, car fan belts, etc.
To raise or rise.
definitions.example: The fog eventually lifted, leaving the streets clear.

definitions.definition: To steal.
To source directly without acknowledgement; to plagiarise.
To arrest (a person).
To remove (a ban, restriction, etc.).
To alleviate, to lighten (pressure, tension, stress, etc.)
To cause to move upwards.
To lift weights; to weight-lift.
definitions.example: She lifts twice a week at the gym.

definitions.definition: To try to raise something; to exert the strength for raising or bearing.
To elevate or improve in rank, condition, etc.; often with up.
To bear; to support.
To collect, as moneys due; to raise.
To transform (a function) into a corresponding function in a different context.
To buy a security or other asset previously offered for sale.
The action or process by which something is lifted; elevation
Weightlifting; a form of exercise in which weights are lifted
Plastic surgery for tightening facial tissues and improving the facial appearance
Theft.
A certain operation on a measure space; see lifting theory.
A means of providing the necessities of life for oneself (for example, a job or income).
definitions.example: an independent livelihood; to make / earn / gain a good livelihood

definitions.definition: Property which brings in an income; an estate.
Liveliness; appearance of life.
The course of someone's life; a person's lifetime, or their manner of living; conduct, behaviour.
Tending to reject.
The act of gerrymandering.
definitions.example: By this iniquitous practice, which is known as the gerrymander, the party in a minority in each State is allowed to get only about one-half or one-quarter of its proper share of representation.

definitions.definition: A voting district skewed by gerrymandering.
definitions.example: Any citizen looking at a map of district 12 could immediately tell that it was a gerrymander because of the ridiculous way it cut across 4 counties while carving up neighborhoods in half.

definitions.definition: To divide a geographic area into voting districts in such a way as to give an unfair advantage to one party in an election.
(by extension) To draw dividing lines for other types of districts in an unintuitive way to favor a particular group or for other perceived gain.
definitions.example: The superintendent helped gerrymander the school district lines in order to keep the children of the wealthy gated community in the better school all the way across town.

definitions.definition: A waterfall or series of small waterfalls.
A stream or sequence of a thing or things occurring as if falling like a cascade.
A series of electrical (or other types of) components, the output of any one being connected to the input of the next; See also daisy chain
A pattern typically performed with an odd number of props, where each prop is caught by the opposite hand.

A sequence of absurd short messages posted to a newsgroup by different authors, each one responding to the most recent message and quoting the entire sequence to that point (with ever-increasing indentation).
A hairpiece for women consisting of curled locks or a bun attached to a firm base, used to create the illusion of fuller hair.
A series of reactions in which the product of one becomes a reactant in the next
To fall as a waterfall or series of small waterfalls.
To arrange in a stepped series like a waterfall.
To occur as a causal sequence.
To vomit.
Someone who is possessed by a demon.
A terrifying and dangerous creature.
A bizarre or whimsical creature.
definitions.example: The children decided Grover was a cuddly monster.

definitions.definition: A cruel or antisocial person, especially a criminal.
definitions.example: Get away from those children, you meatheaded monster!

definitions.definition: A horribly deformed person.
A badly behaved child, a brat.
definitions.example: Sit still, you little monster!

definitions.definition: Something unusually large.
definitions.example: Have you seen those powerlifters on TV? They're monsters.

definitions.definition: A prodigy; someone very talented in a specific domain.
definitions.example: That dude playing guitar is a monster.

definitions.definition: A non-player character that player(s) fight against in role-playing games.
To make into a monster; to categorise as a monster; to demonise.
To behave as a monster to; to terrorise.
To harass.
Very large; worthy of a monster.
definitions.example: He has a monster appetite.

definitions.definition: Great; very good; excellent.
To make better, or improve, something perceived to be in a negative condition.
definitions.example: They offered some compromises in an effort to ameliorate the situation.

definitions.definition: To become better; improve.
(causative) To cause to be or become black.
To become black.
definitions.example: The sky blackened as the storm clouds rolled in.

definitions.definition: (causative) To make dirty.
To defame or sully.
To cook (meat or fish) by coating with pepper, etc., and quickly searing in a hot pan.
A saw with fine teeth and a narrow blade which can cut curves in wood or metal.
A jigsaw puzzle.
To cut something using a jigsaw.
The flat, roundish bone in the knee.
(roofing) A metal cover trim that fits over a panel rib after it has been cut and bent.
A cap or strong covering for the knees, used chiefly for horses, to protect their knees in case of a fall.
Beyond expression in words; unspeakable.
Forbidden to be uttered; taboo.
So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.
definitions.example: High above, vultures circled around.

definitions.definition: So as to surround or be near.
definitions.example: Everybody please gather around.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; about.
definitions.example: An adult elephant weighs around five tons.

definitions.definition: From place to place.
definitions.example: Look around and see what you find.

definitions.definition: From one state or condition to an opposite or very different one; with a metaphorical change in direction; bringing about awareness or agreement.
definitions.example: He used to stay up late but his new girlfriend changed that around.

definitions.definition: (with turn, spin etc.) So as to partially or completely rotate; so as to face in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: She spun around a few times.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate repeated or continuous action, or in numerous locations or with numerous people.
definitions.example: I asked around, and no-one really liked it.

definitions.definition: Used with certain verbs to suggest unproductive activity.
Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.
definitions.example: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.

definitions.definition: (of abstract things) Centred upon; surrounding.
definitions.example: There has been a lot of controversy around the handling of personal information.

definitions.definition: Following the perimeter of a specified area and returning to the starting point.
definitions.example: She went around the track fifty times.

definitions.definition: Following a path which curves near an object, with the object on the inside of the curve.
definitions.example: The road took a brief detour around the large rock formation, then went straight on.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity of.
definitions.example: I don't want you around me.

definitions.definition: At or to various places within.
definitions.example: She went around the office and got everyone to sign the card.

definitions.definition: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.
A circular or repetitious route.
definitions.example: The guards have started their rounds; the prisoner should be caught soon.

definitions.definition: A general outburst from a group of people at an event.
definitions.example: The candidate got a round of applause after every sentence or two.

definitions.definition: A song that is sung by groups of people with each subset of people starting at a different time.
A serving of something; a portion of something to each person in a group.
definitions.example: They brought us a round of drinks about every thirty minutes.

definitions.definition: A single individual portion or dose of medicine.
One sandwich (two full slices of bread with filling).
A long-bristled, circular-headed paintbrush used in oil and acrylic painting.
A firearm cartridge, bullet, or any individual ammunition projectile. Originally referring to the spherical projectile ball of a smoothbore firearm. Compare round shot and solid shot.
One of the specified pre-determined segments of the total time of a sport event, such as a boxing or wrestling match, during which contestants compete before being signaled to stop.
A stage, level, set of events in a game
(drafting, CAD) A rounded relief or cut at an edge, especially an outside edge, added for a finished appearance and to soften sharp edges.
A strip of material with a circular face that covers an edge, gap, or crevice for decorative, sanitary, or security purposes.
definitions.example: All furniture in the nursery had rounds on the edges and in the crevices.

definitions.definition: (butchery) The hindquarters of a bovine.
A rung, as of a ladder.
A crosspiece that joins and braces the legs of a chair.
A series of changes or events ending where it began; a series of like events recurring in continuance; a cycle; a periodical revolution.
definitions.example: the round of the seasons    a round of pleasures

definitions.definition: A course of action or conduct performed by a number of persons in turn, or one after another, as if seated in a circle.
A series of duties or tasks which must be performed in turn, and then repeated.
A circular dance.
Rotation, as in office; succession.
A general discharge of firearms by a body of troops in which each soldier fires once.
An assembly; a group; a circle.
definitions.example: a round of politicians

definitions.definition: A brewer's vessel in which the fermentation is concluded, the yeast escaping through the bunghole.
A vessel filled, as for drinking.
A round-top.
A round of beef.
To shape something into a curve.
definitions.example: The carpenter rounded the edges of the table.

definitions.definition: To become shaped into a curve.
(with "out") To finish; to complete; to fill out.
definitions.example: She rounded out her education with only a single mathematics class.

definitions.definition: To approximate a number, especially a decimal number by the closest whole number.
definitions.example: Ninety-five rounds up to one hundred.

definitions.definition: To turn past a boundary.
definitions.example: Helen watched him until he rounded the corner.

definitions.definition: To turn and attack someone or something (used with on).
definitions.example: As a group of policemen went past him, one of them rounded on him, grabbing him by the arm.

definitions.definition: To advance to home plate.
definitions.example: And the runners round the bases on the double by Jones.

definitions.definition: To go round, pass, go past.
To encircle; to encompass.
To grow round or full; hence, to attain to fullness, completeness, or perfection.
To do ward rounds.
To go round, as a guard; to make the rounds.
To go or turn round; to wheel about.
(physical) Shape.
Complete, whole, not lacking.
definitions.example: The baker sold us a round dozen.

definitions.definition: (of a number) Convenient for rounding other numbers to; for example, ending in a zero.
definitions.example: One hundred is a nice round number.

definitions.definition: Pronounced with the lips drawn together; rounded.
Outspoken; plain and direct; unreserved; not mincing.
definitions.example: a round answer; a round oath

definitions.definition: Finished; polished; not defective or abrupt; said of authors or their writing style.
Consistent; fair; just; applied to conduct.
Large in magnitude.
definitions.example: a round sum

definitions.definition: (authorship, of a fictional character) Well-written and well-characterized; complex and reminiscent of a real person.
Vaulted.
To keep up, impose or bring into effect something, not necessarily by force.
definitions.example: The police are there to enforce the law.

definitions.definition: To give strength or force to; to affirm, to emphasize.
definitions.example: The victim was able to enforce his evidence against the alleged perpetrator.

definitions.definition: To strengthen (a castle, town etc.) with extra troops, fortifications etc.
To intensify, make stronger, add force to.
To exert oneself, to try hard.
To compel, oblige (someone or something); to force.
To make or gain by force; to force.
definitions.example: to enforce a passage

definitions.definition: To put in motion or action by violence; to drive.
To give force to; to strengthen; to invigorate; to urge with energy.
definitions.example: to enforce arguments or requests

definitions.definition: To urge; to ply hard; to lay much stress upon.
To prove; to evince.
An exposed plumbing fitting; a tap or spigot; a regulator for controlling the flow of a liquid from a reservoir.
(game development) One or several systems that inject currency into the game's economy, thus controlling or preventing inflation
Free postage, a right exercised by governments (usually with definite article).
The notice on an envelope where a stamp would normally be found.
To place a frank on an envelope.
To exempt from charge for postage, as a letter, package, or packet, etc.
To send by public conveyance free of expense.
Honest, especially in a manner that seems slightly blunt; candid; not reserved or disguised.
definitions.example: May I be frank with you?

definitions.definition: Unmistakable, clinically obvious, self-evident
definitions.example: The research probes whether treating pre-diabetes with metformin can prevent progression to frank diabetes.

definitions.definition: Unbounded by restrictions, limitations, etc.; free.
Liberal; generous; profuse.
Unrestrained; loose; licentious.
A sharp, hooked claw of a bird of prey or other predatory animal.
One of certain small prominences on the hind part of the face of an elephant's tooth.
A kind of moulding, concave at the bottom and convex at the top; an ogee. (When the concave part is at the top, it is called an inverted talon.)
The shoulder of the bolt of a lock on which the key acts to shoot the bolt.
In various card games, the remaining stock of undealt cards.
One who hunts game for sport or for food; a huntsman or huntswoman.
A dog used in hunting.
A horse used in hunting, especially a thoroughbred, bred and trained for hunting.
One who hunts or seeks after anything.
definitions.example: The hunter becomes the hunted.

definitions.definition: A person who bottles up their aggression and eventually releases it explosively.
A kind of spider, the huntsman or hunting spider.
A pocket watch with a spring-hinged circular metal cover that closes over the dial and crystal, protecting them from dust and scratches.
A coarse mediaeval coat of defence, especially one made of leather.
A man.
A device or utensil.
A non-tool object or thing.
A plant or animal.
The edible fruit of the Asian tree (Artocarpus heterophyllus); also the tree itself.
A home run.
(games) A children's game involving picking up objects; knucklebones; jackstones.
Alternative form of jakes: an outhouse or lavatory.
To bring into being; give rise to.
definitions.example: The discussion generated an uproar.

definitions.definition: To produce as a result of a chemical or physical process.
definitions.example: Adding concentrated sulphuric acid to water generates heat.

definitions.definition: To procreate, beget.
definitions.example: They generated many offspring.

definitions.definition: To form a figure from a curve or solid.
definitions.example: Rotating a circle generates a sphere.

definitions.definition: To appear or occur; be generated.
Someone interested in, or owning a lot of, gadgets
(rarely countable) Unsolicited bulk electronic messages.
definitions.example: I get far too much spam.

definitions.definition: Any undesired electronic content automatically generated for commercial purposes.
A type of tinned meat made mainly from ham.
To send spam (i.e. unsolicited electronic messages.)
To send spam (i.e. unsolicited electronic messages) to a person or entity.
(by extension) To use (a spell or ability) rapidly and repeatedly.
definitions.example: Stop spamming that special attack!

definitions.definition: To post the same text repeatedly with disruptive effect; to flood.
To incite an attack by, especially a dog or dogs.
definitions.example: He sicced his dog on me!

definitions.definition: To set upon; to chase; to attack.
To vomit.
definitions.example: I woke up at 4 am and sicked on the floor.

definitions.definition: To fall sick; to sicken.
To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.
To weaken or work against; to hinder, sabotage.
definitions.example: The war efforts were undermined by the constant bickering between the allies.

definitions.definition: To erode the base or foundation of something, e.g. by the action of water.
Showing little or no feeling.
definitions.example: An unemotional person

definitions.definition: Reasoned and objective, involving reason or intellect rather than feelings.
A Standard ballroom dance in 4/4 time; or a social dance, the Argentine tango.
A piece of music suited to such a dance.
The letter T in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
Enemy, used amongst special police forces, derived from the abbreviation of target using the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A dark orange colour shade; deep tangerine
To dance the tango.
To mingle or interact (with each other).
Of, or relating to, ontology.
Of or pertaining to the nature of being or existence.
A liquor distilled from the fermented mash of grain (as rye, corn, or barley).
A drink of whiskey.

An organized group comprising people and/or equipment.
definitions.example: He was a member of a special police unit.

definitions.definition: A member of a military organization.
definitions.example: The fifth tank brigade moved in with 20 units. (i.e., 20 tanks)

definitions.definition: Any military element whose structure is prescribed by competent authority, such as a table of organization and equipment; specifically, part of an organization.
An organization title of a subdivision of a group in a task force.
A standard or basic quantity into which an item of supply is divided, issued or detailed. In this meaning, also called unit of issue.

With regard to Reserve Components of the Armed Forces, denotes a Selected Reserve unit organized, equipped, and trained for mobilization to serve on active duty as a unit or to augment or be augmented by another unit. Headquarters and support functions without wartime missions are not considered units.
The identity element, neutral element.
An element having an inverse, an invertible element; an associate of the unity.
In an adjunction, a natural transformation from the identity functor of the domain of the left adjoint functor to the composition of the right adjoint functor with the left adjoint functor.

A volume of rock or ice of identifiable origin and age range that is defined by the distinctive and dominant, easily mapped and recognizable petrographic, lithologic or paleontologic features (facies) that characterize it.
An item which may be sold singly.
definitions.example: We shipped nearly twice as many units this month as last month.

definitions.definition: A unit of alcohol.
One kilowatt-hour (as recorded on an electricity meter).

A measure of housing equivalent to the living quarters of one household; an apartment where a group of apartments is contained in one or more multi-storied buildings or a group of dwellings is in one or more single storey buildings, usually arranged around a driveway.
A gold coin of the reign of James I, worth twenty shillings.
A work unit.
A physically large person.
For each unit.
definitions.example: We have to keep our unit costs down if we want to make a profit.

definitions.definition: Having a size or magnitude of one.
A cave or cavern.
An underground vault, especially one beneath a church that is used as a burial place.
A small pit or cavity in the surface of an organ or other structure.
Able to be made or modified by machine.
Flair, vitality, or zest; energy; vigor.
definitions.example: The show had a lot of pizzazz, with glittering costumes and upbeat music.

definitions.definition: Ability, skill, competence.
definitions.example: a test of proficiency in English

definitions.definition: To raise using a jack.
definitions.example: He jacked the car up so that he could replace the brake pads.

definitions.definition: To raise or increase.
definitions.example: If you want to jack your stats you just write off failures as invalid results.

definitions.definition: To produce by freeze distillation; to distil (an alcoholic beverage) by freezing it and removing the ice (which is water), leaving the alcohol (which remains liquid).
To steal something, typically an automobile. Shortened form of carjacking.
definitions.example: Someone jacked my car last night!

definitions.definition: To dance by moving the torso forward and backward in a rippling motion.
To hit (the ball) hard; especially, to hit (the ball) out of the field, producing a home run.
High on drugs or stimulants.
Broken, imperfect, especially when followed by 'up'. (See jacked up)
definitions.example: This computer is so jacked up, I'm surprised it can still turn on!

definitions.definition: Strong and/or muscled.
definitions.example: Wow, that guy is jacked!
